channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Minneapolis City Council member Emily Koski joins mayor’s race
item.description: She joins Minnesota DFL Sen. Omar Fateh and the Rev. DeWayne Davis of Plymouth Congregational Church in challenging Mayor Jacob Frey, who’s running for reelection.
item.dc:creator: Megan Burks, MPR News

item.title: Trump criticizes Chronister after sheriff's withdrawal, days after praising him
item.description: President Donald Trump challenged reports of Hillsborough County Sheriff Chad Chronister's DEA withdrawal, saying he didn't want the sheriff days his cabinet days after nominating him.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Rodriguez, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Reporter's Notebook: People are policy
item.description: This week begins the quadrennial tradition of various Cabinet nominees parading around the Senate to meet with lawmakers, answer questions, and get insight into their confirmation hearing.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden appears to rest his eyes at African summit in Angola
item.description: President Biden appeared to rest his eyes during a summit with African leaders in Lobito, Angola, during his travel overseas to visit African nations.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Talking Sense: Coming together to solve tough problems
item.description: How do people come together in a divided political environment to solve tough problems? Coming up at 9 a.m. on Thursday, tune in for our most recent MPR News Talking Sense conversation about how people can come together to find common ground.
item.dc:creator: Angela Davis, Catharine Richert, Maja Beckstrom, and Annie Baxter, MPR News

item.title: Supreme Court leans toward upholding Tennessee's restrictions on gender-affirming care
item.description: The Supreme Court’s conservatives appeared to lean toward upholding Tennessee’s ban on gender-affirming care for minors in a blockbuster showdown over transgender rights Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Outgoing GOP congressman seeks role as Trump's drug czar after first nominee falls through
item.description: Outgoing GOP Rep. Anthony D'Esposito, R-N.Y., is gunning to be the next leader of President-elect Donald Trump's Drug Enforcement Agency, after Trump's first nominee withdrew his name amid criticism.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Las Vegas Mayor Shelley Berkley sworn into office; envisions future of prosperity for the city
item.description: Shelley Berkley was sworn in as the mayor of Las Vegas on Wednesday, promising to serve the city well and work with the community to create an atmosphere of growth and prosperity.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Bleakley, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump names new point person for negotiating the release of hostages, detainees abroad
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump on Wednesday named a health care executive to serve as his administration's lead hostage negotiator at a time when the U.S. is trying to secure the release of prisoners in Gaza and other regions around the world.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump nominates cryptocurrency advocate Paul Atkins as SEC chair
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump says he intends to nominate cryptocurrency advocate Paul Atkins to chair the Securities and Exchange Commission.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump seeks to have Georgia election case dismissed, citing presidential immunity
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump is trying to get the Georgia election interference case against him dismissed.
item.dc:creator: Kate Brumback, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Detroit Mayor Duggan, a longtime Democrat, will run for Michigan governor in 2026 as independent
item.description: DETROIT (AP) — Detroit Mayor Mike Duggan, a longtime Democrat, says he will run for Michigan governor as an independent in 2026. “It’s clear to me that there are a lot of people in this country who are tired of both parties and tired of the system," Duggan said in an interview Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: COREY WILLIAMS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump announces picks for Army secretary, trade adviser, hostage envoy, NASA administrator and more
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump announced several new additions to his incoming administration on Wednesday, including his selections for Army secretary and NASA adminstrator.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hegseth faces growing GOP scrutiny as allegations mount
item.description: Correction: President-elect Trump announced his nomination of Pete Hegseth for Defense Secretary on Nov. 12. A previous version of this article contained incorrect information.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, NewsNation

item.title: Trump transition signs MOU with DOJ to facilitate FBI background checks
item.description: President-elect Trump’s transition team announced Tuesday it had signed a memorandum of understanding (MOU) with the Justice Department, which will allow the FBI to conduct background checks for certain individuals chosen for the incoming administration.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Trump floats DeSantis as potential defense secretary replacement if Hegseth falters
item.description: President-elect Trump is considering nominating Republican Gov. Ron DeSantis of Florida as defense secretary to replace Pete Hegseth, Trump's embattled first pick to steer the Pentagon, according to sources.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump reportedly considering DeSantis as replacement nominee for defense secretary
item.description: Several reports, including from the Wall Street Journal, have said Governor Ron DeSantis could become President-elect Trump's new defense secretary nominee. The news comes amid a growing number of allegations against Trump's original nominee Pete Hegseth.
item.dc:creator: Beth Rousseau, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Supreme Court hears challenge to law banning gender-affirming care for trans kids
item.description: At issue is a Tennessee law that bans access to hormones, puberty blockers, and other treatments for trans kids in the state.
item.dc:creator: Nina Totenberg, MPR News

item.title: Condo deadline looms: Owners must confront years of neglect
item.description: The condo conundrum continues as the December 31st inspection deadline is only a few weeks away. State lawmakers promise action on the issue next session, but the clock is ticking.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump and Republican senators plan agenda for first 30 days
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump dialed in to what was described as a “love fest” Tuesday with Senate Republicans as they begin laying the groundwork for control of government in the new year, and a potential first-30-days agenda.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO and MARY CLARE JALONICK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Chronister's withdrawal highlights how incoming presidents often lose Cabinet nominees
item.description: MARTIN, Tenn. (AP) — Losing a Cabinet nominee to the confirmation process isn't unheard of for incoming presidents — including for Donald Trump when he was elected the first time.
item.dc:creator: MEG KINNARD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge closes Hunter Biden gun case after presidential pardon
item.description: Hunter Biden’s federal gun case was brought to a close by a judge Tuesday following the presidential pardon by his father. In a terse order, U.S. District Judge Maryellen Noreika terminated all proceedings in the case, citing President Biden’s executive grant of clemency to his son.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: Minnesota House panel votes to absorb legal fees of DFLer in employment dispute
item.description: A DFL state lawmaker will have legal fees covered by the Minnesota House after alleging her rights were violated by an outside employer. Republicans say the move sets a bad precedent.
item.dc:creator: Brian Bakst, MPR News

item.title: Ocasio-Cortez first person to hit a million followers on Bluesky
item.description: Other than Bluesky’s own account, Ocasio-Cortez’s follower landmark is a first for a user, the platform told The Hill.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: Pressley: Biden 'must act' on clemency before leaving office
item.description: Rep. Ayanna Pressley (D-Mass.) is renewing calls for President Biden to grant clemency to thousands of Americans serving time in federal institutions following his bombshell pardon of his son, Hunter Biden.
item.dc:creator: Cheyanne M. Daniels, NewsNation

item.title: Biden’s pardon of son Hunter threatens to tarnish legacy
item.description: President Biden’s decision to pardon his son Hunter in his final full month in the White House is threatening to erode his legacy as he prepares to leave office.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: At Supreme Court, vaping may be frowned upon, but that could change with Trump
item.description: The focal point of the case is 2009 law enacted by Congress that gives the Food and Drug Administration a mandate to curb the availability of nicotine products for minors.
item.dc:creator: Nina Totenberg, MPR News

item.title: White House says Trump wouldn't 'let go' of Hunter Biden in defending pardon
item.description: The White House on Monday defended President Biden’s decision to pardon his son Hunter Biden after insisting for more than a year that he wouldn’t do so.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation
